015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.http.part;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
022
023import java.util.*;
024import java.util.function.*;
025
026/**
027 * An {@link org.apache.juneau.http.HttpPart} whose value is a comma-delimited list of strings.
028 *
029 * <p>
030 * Mirrors the semantics of {@code BasicCsvArrayPart} from {@code juneau-rest-common-classic} without
031 * the Apache HttpCore dependency.
032 *
033 * @since 10.0.0
034 */
035@SuppressWarnings({
036   "java:S2160" // Equality is fully determined by name + getValue() in HttpPartBean; the typed fields are derived from the wire value and add no state to compare
037})
038public class HttpCsvArrayPart extends HttpPartBean {
039
040   private static final String[] EMPTY = {};
041
042   /**
043    * Creates an {@link HttpCsvArrayPart} with the given name and array value.
044    *
045    * @param name The part name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
046    * @param value The array of values. May be <jk>null</jk>.
047    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
048    */
049   public static HttpCsvArrayPart of(String name, String...value) {
050      return new HttpCsvArrayPart(name, value);
051   }
052
053   /**
054    * Creates an {@link HttpCsvArrayPart} by parsing the given comma-delimited wire value.
055    *
056    * @param name The part name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
057    * @param value The wire value. May be <jk>null</jk> or empty.
058    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
059    */
060   public static HttpCsvArrayPart ofString(String name, String value) {
061      return new HttpCsvArrayPart(name, value);
062   }
063
064   /**
065    * Creates an {@link HttpCsvArrayPart} with a lazy array supplier.
066    *
067    * @param name The part name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
068    * @param supplier Supplier for the array value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
069    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
070    */
071   public static HttpCsvArrayPart ofLazy(String name, Supplier<String[]> supplier) {
072      return new HttpCsvArrayPart(name, supplier);
073   }
074
075   private final String[] typedValue;
076   private final Supplier<String[]> typedSupplier;
077
078   /**
079    * Constructor accepting an array of values.
080    *
081    * @param name The part name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
082    * @param value The array of values. May be <jk>null</jk>.
083    */
084   public HttpCsvArrayPart(String name, String...value) {
085      super(name, value == null ? null : join(value, ','));
086      this.typedValue = value;
087      this.typedSupplier = null;
088   }
089
090   /**
091    * Constructor accepting a comma-delimited wire string.
092    *
093    * @param name The part name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
094    * @param value The wire value. May be <jk>null</jk> or empty.
095    */
096   public HttpCsvArrayPart(String name, String value) {
097      super(name, value);
098      this.typedValue = splita(value);
099      this.typedSupplier = null;
100   }
101
102   /**
103    * Constructor accepting a lazy supplier of array values.
104    *
105    * @param name The part name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
106    * @param supplier Supplier for the array value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
107    */
108   public HttpCsvArrayPart(String name, Supplier<String[]> supplier) {
109      super(name, () -> {
110         var v = supplier.get();
111         return v == null ? null : join(v, ',');
112      });
113      this.typedValue = null;
114      this.typedSupplier = supplier;
115   }
116
117   /**
118    * Returns the value as a copy of the underlying array, wrapped in an {@link Optional}.
119    *
120    * @return The array value, wrapped in an {@link Optional}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
121    */
122   public Optional<String[]> asArray() {
123      return opt(toArray());
124   }
125
126   /**
127    * Returns the value as an unmodifiable {@link List}, wrapped in an {@link Optional}.
128    *
129    * @return The list value, wrapped in an {@link Optional}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
130    */
131   public Optional<List<String>> asList() {
132      return opt(toList());
133   }
134
135   /**
136    * Returns {@code true} if this part contains the given value (case-sensitive).
137    *
138    * @param val The value to check for.
139    * @return {@code true} if the value is present.
140    */
141   public boolean contains(String val) {
142      if (val == null)
143         return false;
144      var arr = value();
145      if (arr == null)
146         return false;
147      for (var v : arr)
148         if (eq(v, val))
149            return true;
150      return false;
151   }
152
153   /**
154    * Returns {@code true} if this part contains the given value (case-insensitive).
155    *
156    * @param val The value to check for.
157    * @return {@code true} if the value is present.
158    */
159   public boolean containsIgnoreCase(String val) {
160      if (val == null)
161         return false;
162      var arr = value();
163      if (arr == null)
164         return false;
165      for (var v : arr)
166         if (eqic(v, val))
167            return true;
168      return false;
169   }
170
171   /**
172    * Returns a copy of the underlying array, or {@code null} if not set.
173    *
174    * @return The array, possibly {@code null}.
175    */
176   public String[] toArray() {
177      return copyOf(value());
178   }
179
180   /**
181    * Returns the value as an unmodifiable {@link List}, or {@code null} if not set.
182    *
183    * @return The list, possibly {@code null}.
184    */
185   public List<String> toList() {
186      return u(l(value()));
187   }
188
189   /**
190    * Returns the value if present, otherwise returns {@code other}.
191    *
192    * @param other The default value.
193    * @return The value or {@code other}.
194    */
195   public String[] orElse(String[] other) {
196      var x = value();
197      return nn(x) ? x : other;
198   }
199
200   private String[] value() {
201      if (typedSupplier != null) {
202         var v = typedSupplier.get();
203         return v == null ? EMPTY : v;
204      }
205      return typedValue;
206   }
207}
